var_functions.query_db:2.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

var_functions.query_db:2.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. args.code:
import json
import re

# Get business info from the stored result
business_info = locals()['var_functions.query_db:16']

if business_info and len(business_info) > 0:
    business = business_info[0]
    name = business.get('name', 'Unknown')
    description = business.get('description', '')
    
    # Extract categories from description
    # The description mentions categories at the end: "Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch, American (New), Cafes"
    print(f'Business name: {name}')
    print(f'Description: {description}')
    
    # Try to extract the category part from the description
    # The pattern is usually: "perfect for a cozy meal any time of the day." followed by categories
    # or "featuring Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch, ..."
    
    # Extract the part after "featuring" or "offers" or the last sentence that contains the categories
    category_patterns = [
        r'featuring\s+([^\.]+)',
        r'offers\s+a\s+delightful[^\.]*\s+featuring\s+([^\.]+)',
        r'perfect for[^\.]*\.\s*([^\.]+)$'
    ]
    
    categories = []
    for pattern in category_patterns:
        match = re.search(pattern, description, re.IGNORECASE)
        if match:
            categories_text = match.group(1).strip()
            # Remove trailing period if present
            if categories_text.endswith('.'):
                categories_text = categories_text[:-1]
            categories = [cat.strip() for cat in categories_text.split(',')]
            break
    
    if not categories and 'featuring' in description:
        # Fallback: extract everything after "featuring"
        parts = description.split('featuring')
        if len(parts) > 1:
            category_part = parts[1].strip()
            if category_part.endswith('.'):
                category_part = category_part[:-1]
            categories = [cat.strip() for cat in category_part.split(',')]
    
    print(f'Categories found: {categories}')
    
    # Get average rating info
    top_business_info = locals()['var_functions.execute_python:14']
    top_business = json.loads(top_business_info).get('top_business', {})
    
    result = {
        'business_name': name,
        'avg_rating': top_business.get('avg_rating'),
        'review_count': top_business.get('review_count'),
        'categories': categories
    }
    
    print('__RESULT__:')
    print(json.dumps(result))
else:
    print('No business found')
    print('__RESULT__:')
    print(json.dumps({'error': 'No business found'}))

args.code:
import json

# Get the result from previous execution
result = locals()['var_functions.execute_python:20']

# Clean up the categories - remove the descriptive phrase that got included
if 'categories' in result:
    # Filter out the descriptive phrase - keep only actual categories
    # Categories should be short, not long sentences
    cleaned_categories = []
    for cat in result['categories']:
        # If it's a long phrase (likely descriptive text), skip it
        if len(cat) > 50 or 'perfect for' in cat or 'any time of the day' in cat:
            continue
        cleaned_categories.append(cat)
    
    result['categories'] = cleaned_categories

# Format the final answer
business_name = result.get('business_name', 'Unknown')
avg_rating = result.get('avg_rating', 0)
review_count = result.get('review_count', 0)
categories = result.get('categories', [])

final_answer = f"The business with the highest average rating between January 1, 2016 and June 30, 2016 is '{business_name}' with an average rating of {avg_rating} based on {review_count} reviews. It belongs to the following categories: {', '.join(categories)}."

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({'final_answer': final_answer}))

var_functions.query_db:2.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened
